spec_match.rationale: Tests exact fragment matching behavior using three standards-mode anchors and a real no-doctype iframe whose contentDocument.compatMode is BackCompat, matching the document mode affected by the Chrome 150 change.

interactive.rationale: Users can click #section, #Section, #SECTION, and #MISSING to drive the same fragment through both documents, with live readouts for standards and quirks targets.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now separates this browser's actual BackCompat result from the Chrome 150+ expected result, so older Chrome builds show fallback behavior without claiming support.

edge_cases.rationale: The repaired page creates a no-doctype iframe with contentDocument.open/write/close, verifies BackCompat, covers mixed/lower/upper case and missing fragments, and labels Chrome <150 fallback targeting separately.

design_system.rationale: Excellent responsive layout, consistent CSS classes, and fits seamlessly into the design style rules.

summary: Repaired with Chrome DevTools MCP evidence: before the fix the page had zero iframes and only ran in CSS1Compat; after the fix the fragment links exercise one CSS1Compat document and one BackCompat iframe. Chrome 148 showed exact standards targets, old BackCompat fallback for #Section/#SECTION, no target for #MISSING, no console errors, and the updated conformance route rendered 7 PASS / 0 FAIL.
